steps.title: Use two-variable coefficient regression
steps.content: The answer choices are quadratic expressions in $x$ and $y$. Instead of expanding every term by hand, use Desmos to fit the expression to the form $ax^2+bxy+cy^2$.

steps.title: Evaluate the expression at three points
steps.content:
Enter three simple $(x,y)$ test points and compute the expression values:
$$x_1=[1,0,1]$$
$$y_1=[0,1,1]$$
$$z_1=(x_1-11y_1)(2x_1-3y_1)-12y_1(-2x_1+3y_1)$$

steps.title: Fit the coefficient model
steps.content:
Now enter:
$$z_1\sim ax_1^2+bx_1y_1+cy_1^2$$
Desmos returns $a=2$, $b=-1$, and $c=-3$. So the equivalent expression is:
$$2x^2-xy-3y^2$$

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: $2x^2-xy-3y^2$ matches choice B. Expanding manually confirms it: $(x-11y)(2x-3y)=2x^2-25xy+33y^2$, and $-12y(-2x+3y)=24xy-36y^2$, giving $2x^2-xy-3y^2$.
